business_meaning: Share of annual volume that fails on the legacy path before any intervention.
reason_for_range: Uses the public benchmark's rough P10, median, and P90 failure rates as an external anchor for a critical digital transaction path while keeping the domain mismatch explicit.
source_reference: HM Land Registry live service data for Search (gov), March 2023 to March 2024. Modeled failure rate is 1 minus the published completion rate.

business_meaning: Direct operational cost created by one failed unit.
reason_for_range: Captures the difference between cheap retries and expensive manual recovery.
source_reference: Synthetic support-and-recovery cost range for a commerce platform.

business_meaning: Annual internal operating drag of staying on the current path, including firefighting, coordination overhead, and workaround cost but excluding customer-side revenue loss.
reason_for_range: Keeps status quo deterioration visible without blending the same customer harm already captured through failure-linked value loss.
source_reference: Placeholder for observed internal drift cost in a live product, such as manual recovery, incident handling, and engineering overhead.

business_meaning: Failure-exposure reduction for users who adopt the express path.
reason_for_range: Assumes the extension avoids a meaningful but not total share of the problematic path.
source_reference: Portfolio assumption about how much of the legacy flow the extension bypasses.

business_meaning: Share of extension value that leaks away through disablement or decay.
reason_for_range: Prevents the extension case from assuming perfect value retention.
source_reference: Placeholder for observed disablement or value-decay telemetry in a real product.

business_meaning: Share of users who adopt the optional express path.
reason_for_range: Keeps the extension economically sensitive to adoption rather than assuming it.
source_reference: Synthetic feature-adoption range for an optional convenience flow.

business_meaning: Additional value created by one adopting unit before decay or disablement.
reason_for_range: Keeps the extension additive without letting convenience dominate reliability economics.
source_reference: Portfolio assumption for how much convenience value the extension creates per adopter.

business_meaning: Relative downstream value loss that follows when one failure damages trust, retention, or future conversion for the affected unit.
reason_for_range: Keeps customer-harm effects visible without forcing every failure to become full churn.
source_reference: Portfolio case judgment for how often an exposed failure causes customer-side value loss beyond the direct failure cost.

business_meaning: Ongoing annual cost of operating the express path.
reason_for_range: Reflects extra ongoing complexity from keeping the extension alive.
source_reference: Portfolio assumption for supporting an additional production path.

business_meaning: Time needed for extension adoption and operational impact to reach full effect.
reason_for_range: Prevents the model from assuming full uptake on the day the extension launches.
source_reference: Portfolio assumption for adoption ramp after shipping an optional convenience flow.

business_meaning: Delivery-cost multiplier applied to the feature-extension upfront cost.
reason_for_range: Keeps the extension from appearing cheaper than its coordination burden in every world.
source_reference: Portfolio assumption for moderate delivery variance on a contained product build.

business_meaning: Time before the express-path investment begins producing production benefits.
reason_for_range: Keeps the extension faster than a core refactor, but not instant.
source_reference: Synthetic delivery estimate for a bounded optional-flow launch.

business_meaning: Relative release-risk multiplier for the feature-extension option.
reason_for_range: Lets the extension carry risk, but slightly less than the baseline refactor.
source_reference: Portfolio assumption that the extension is easier to contain than a core refactor.

business_meaning: One-time investment required to ship the express path.
reason_for_range: Keeps the extension cheaper than the refactor but still material.
source_reference: Synthetic budget line for an optional express-flow build.

business_meaning: Ongoing annual cost of supporting the new capability.
reason_for_range: Makes the capability carry visible ownership cost after launch.
source_reference: Portfolio assumption for operating a more complex capability over time.

business_meaning: Time needed for the new capability to ramp from launch to full effect.
reason_for_range: Avoids treating a new capability as instantly mature once it ships.
source_reference: Portfolio assumption for controlled rollout, tuning, and trust-building after launch.

business_meaning: Delivery-cost multiplier applied to the new-capability upfront cost.
reason_for_range: Makes the new capability pay for the extra complexity it introduces.
source_reference: Portfolio assumption for higher delivery variance on a new operational capability.

business_meaning: Time before the new capability begins producing production benefits.
reason_for_range: Reflects the longer path from build to trusted production value for a new capability.
source_reference: Synthetic delivery estimate for a new fraud-prevention capability with integration work.

business_meaning: Relative release-risk multiplier for the new-capability option.
reason_for_range: Keeps the new capability from looking like free upside.
source_reference: Portfolio assumption that the new capability adds the most operational complexity.

business_meaning: One-time investment required to build the new capability.
reason_for_range: Makes the option clearly more ambitious and harder to justify.
source_reference: Synthetic budget line for a new capability with model and integration work.

business_meaning: Incremental value uplift created by the new capability on successful volume.
reason_for_range: Allows upside while still forcing the option to earn back delivery complexity.
source_reference: Portfolio assumption for fraud-prevention upside in a high-variance build.

business_meaning: Severity of one regression event when it happens.
reason_for_range: Gives the release-risk model a visible tail instead of collapsing it into expected cost.
source_reference: Synthetic heavy-tail incident-cost assumption for a revenue-critical product flow.

business_meaning: Baseline probability that a shipped option triggers a regression event in one simulated world.
reason_for_range: Wide enough to keep downside tails visible without making every option catastrophically unsafe.
source_reference: Synthetic release-risk range for product changes touching critical flows.

business_meaning: Ongoing annual cost of supporting the stabilized core.
reason_for_range: Keeps the option from looking like a one-off cost with no carry.
source_reference: Portfolio assumption for follow-on maintenance after the main refactor.

business_meaning: Time needed for the stabilize-core option to reach full operational effect after launch.
reason_for_range: Avoids granting full reliability recovery from the first month after release.
source_reference: Portfolio rollout assumption for progressive remediation and validation.

business_meaning: Delivery-cost multiplier applied to the stabilize-core upfront cost.
reason_for_range: Lets the stability program pay for schedule and coordination complexity when it slips.
source_reference: Portfolio assumption for refactor delivery risk in a critical platform surface.

business_meaning: Time before the stabilize-core investment begins producing production benefits.
reason_for_range: Captures schedule risk without assuming the refactor slips indefinitely.
source_reference: Synthetic delivery estimate for a multi-quarter checkout hardening program.

business_meaning: Relative release-risk multiplier for the stabilize-core option.
reason_for_range: Treats the core refactor as the release-risk baseline.
source_reference: Portfolio baseline for a high-touch refactor.

business_meaning: One-time investment required to stabilize the core flow.
reason_for_range: Chosen as a large but plausible spend for a two-year platform program.
source_reference: Synthetic budget line for a major checkout refactor.

business_meaning: Share of baseline failure exposure removed by the core-stability option.
reason_for_range: Broad enough to reflect uncertainty in refactor effectiveness across legacy paths.
source_reference: Synthetic engineering judgment for the scope of a checkout hardening program.

business_meaning: Total economic value created when one unit succeeds.
reason_for_range: Keeps the same success-value term available to both recovery and uplift economics.
source_reference: Synthetic unit-value range for a mid-market commerce flow.
